relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: YHWH speaks the tending of light and presence.
Pure oil keeps the lamp rising continually.
Aaron tends the light from evening to morning before YHWH.
The lamps remain ordered upon the pure lampstand.
Twelve loaves are baked from fine flour.
The loaves are set in two rows before YHWH.
Frankincense makes the bread a memorial before the holy.
Sabbath by Sabbath the bread is renewed as covenant.
The holy bread becomes provision for Aaron and his sons.
A conflict in the camp brings a mixed-heritage man before the community.
The Name is cursed, and the case is brought to Moses rather than answered instantly.
The accused is held while the people seek YHWH’s judgment.
YHWH speaks the judgment.
The ancient sentence is severe and communal.
Cursing the Divine is named as carried sin/misalignment.
Native and sojourner are placed under one ancient covenant law.
Human life is guarded with the strongest penalty language.
Animal loss requires restitution.
Bodily injury is answered proportionally, not escalated.
Fracture, eye, and tooth language limits retaliation to the measure of harm.
Animal harm is repaid; human killing is treated with grave severity.
One judgment applies to native and sojourner because YHWH is Divine of all the covenant community.
The severe ancient judgment is carried out as commanded.

contemplative_note: Leviticus 24 holds two very different rhythms together: continual light and bread before YHWH, then a severe legal case. The safest reading carries forward presence, due process, equal law, proportional restraint, and a categorical refusal to reenact ancient violence today.
